initiative: SBI Holdings in talks to acquire stake in crypto exchange Bitbank, eyes subsidiary status
what_happened: SBI Holdings has submitted a letter of intent to acquire a controlling stake in Bitbank, one of Japan's larger domestic crypto exchanges, with the goal of making it a consolidated subsidiary — a structurally significant threshold that would bring Bitbank onto SBI's balance sheet and under its regulatory perimeter. The move follows SBI's absorption of Bitpoint Japan into its in-house crypto arm, SBI VC Trade, last month, indicating a deliberate strategy to consolidate Japan's fragmented retail crypto exchange landscape under a single group umbrella ahead of anticipated regulatory tightening as crypto assets move toward coverage under the Financial Instruments and Exchange Act.

initiative: Philippines: dYdX among 7 ‘crypto’ platforms flagged by SEC
what_happened: The Philippine SEC published a formal investor alert naming dYdX alongside AEVO, GTrade, Pacifica, Orderly, Deriv, and Ostium as unregistered platforms soliciting investments from Filipino residents without the licenses required under the country's Crypto-Asset Service Provider (CASP) Rules, which mandate registration for any entity—domestic or foreign—offering crypto-asset services to persons in the Philippines. None of the seven platforms hold SEC registration or authorization, exposing them and any local promoters to penalties under the Securities Regulation Code of up to PHP 5 million (approximately USD 89,000) and up to 21 years' imprisonment.

initiative: Fed pick Warsh backs crypto’s place in finance as Warren raises ‘sock puppet’ concerns in hearing
what_happened: Kevin Warsh, President Trump's nominee to chair the Federal Reserve, appeared before the Senate Banking Committee for his confirmation hearing and affirmed that digital assets are already embedded in the U.S. financial system, signaling a materially more accommodative posture toward crypto at the Fed than under current Chair Jerome Powell.

initiative: Kraken parent to pay $550m for CFTC-licensed derivatives platform Bitnomial
what_happened: The parent company of crypto exchange Kraken has struck a $550 million deal to buy Bitnomial, a fully CFTC-licensed derivatives platform built for digital assets.

initiative: A year under Paul Atkins: SEC crypto embrace grows as prediction markets draw scrutiny
what_happened: Nearly one year into his tenure, SEC Chair Paul Atkins publicly reaffirmed the agency's strategic reorientation toward crypto under his "ACT" (Advance, Clarify, Transform) framework, explicitly distancing the SEC from the enforcement-first posture pursued under Gary Gensler—during which the agency pursued numerous actions against major crypto firms on the basis that most digital assets constitute securities, many of which have since been withdrawn. Atkins highlighted "Project Crypto" and an upcoming innovation exemption as concrete regulatory outputs designed to repatriate offshore digital asset activity back to U.S. jurisdiction.

initiative: UK lawmaker urges FCA probe of Farage crypto promotion
what_happened: Liberal Democrat Deputy Leader Daisy Cooper formally wrote to FCA CEO Nikhil Rathi requesting an investigation into Nigel Farage's participation in a promotional video for Aquis-listed Stack BTC, in which the company announced Farage had executed a £2 million Bitcoin purchase on its behalf — weeks after he had personally invested £215,000 in the firm. Cooper's complaint centers on potential market abuse under UK market manipulation rules, conflict of interest, and consumer harm, drawing a parallel to Bloomberg's findings that Trump and associates extracted $1.4 billion from crypto ventures while shaping favorable regulation.

initiative: Thailand eyes stricter checks on crypto firm backers
what_happened: Thailand SEC Proposes Expanded Major Shareholder Definition for Crypto and Securities Firms Thailand's Securities and Exchange Commission has opened a public consultation, closing April 22, on draft rules that would classify funding providers and financial backers of existing major shareholders as major shareholders themselves, thereby subjecting them to the same formal SEC approval requirements. The proposal follows March 2026 rule changes that tightened indirect ownership tracing through corporate chains and expanded the definition of controlling persons to include spouses, cohabitants, minor children, and parties acting in concert on voting rights, which took effect March 4 with a 180-day compliance window for operators to submit approval requests.

initiative: CME is set to let traders bet on bitcoin volatility, not just price
what_happened: (CoinDesk Archives) What to know : CME Group plans to launch bitcoin volatility futures on June 1 pending regulatory approval bringing a easy way to bet on the degree of price swings. The world's leading derivatives marketplace announced this week its plan to debut Bitcoin volatility futures on June 1, pending regulatory approval. initiative: CME Group to launch cash-settled bitcoin volatility futures
what_happened: CME Group announced it will list Bitcoin Volatility futures (ticker: BVI) on June 1, 2026, pending CFTC regulatory approval, making it the first such product available on a regulated U.S. exchange. The cash-settled contracts will carry a notional multiplier of $500 times the CME CF Bitcoin Volatility Index (BVX), which measures 30-day implied volatility derived in real time from CME's existing CFTC-regulated Bitcoin and Bitcoin Micro options order books — infrastructure that CF Benchmarks and CME jointly formalized in December 2024.

initiative: Coinbase Australia launches crypto support for self-directed retirement funds
what_happened: Coinbase Australia has formally extended its platform to serve self-managed superannuation fund (SMSF) trustees, a move enabled by the Australian Financial Services Licence the exchange secured just weeks prior. The offering includes ATO-aligned accounting data exports and an entity verification workflow adapted to SMSF legal structures, addressing the specific compliance and audit requirements that have historically made institutional-grade crypto access difficult for this vehicle. SMSFs are regulated directly by the Australian Taxation Office and collectively held approximately AU$1.06 trillion across roughly 664,000 funds at end-2025, representing a substantial addressable pool of retirement capital that is already legally permitted to hold crypto.

initiative: Australian regulator warns of ungoverned AI risks to banks
what_happened: The Australian Prudential Regulation Authority (Apra) has called for a step-change in how banks, insurers and superannuation trustees manage AI-related risks as the technology continues to rapidly evolve.

initiative: Identification of Critical Functions of Insurers: Practices paper – Revised version
what_happened: The Financial Stability Board has released a revised version of its 2023 practices paper on identifying critical functions of insurers, updated to reflect a new definition of "critical function" introduced in the FSB's concurrently finalised guidance on the scope of insurers subject to recovery and resolution planning under the Key Attributes framework. The revision draws on approaches from four jurisdictions — Australia, China, France, and the Netherlands — documenting how each has developed or advanced methodology for scoping which insurer functions are sufficiently vital to the real economy that their disruption would warrant resolution intervention.

initiative: Key Attributes Assessment Methodology for the Insurance Sector: Revised version
what_happened: The Financial Stability Board published a revised version of its Key Attributes Assessment Methodology for the Insurance Sector on 29 April 2026, updating the 2020 original to incorporate changes stemming from newly finalized guidance on which insurers fall within the scope of recovery and resolution planning requirements. The revision reflects the FSB's 2022 decision—reaffirmed in November 2025—to discontinue annual identification of global systemically important insurers (G-SIIs) and instead rely on the IAIS Holistic Framework to assess systemic risk, a structural shift that necessitated an updated definition of "critical function" embedded in the methodology via an explanatory note.

initiative: Developing Effective Resolution Strategies and Plans for Systemically Important Insurers: Revised version
what_happened: The Financial Stability Board has released a revised version of its 2016 guidance on resolution strategies and plans for systemically important insurers, updated to incorporate changes to the definition of "critical functions" introduced through a companion scoping paper finalized simultaneously. The revised guidance is directed at national resolution authorities and Crisis Management Groups overseeing global systemically important insurers, setting out how preferred resolution strategies should be determined by analyzing business models, policyholder protection arrangements, and the criticality of specific insurance functions.

initiative: EU’s 20th Russia Sanctions Package Signals a New Era of Crypto-Specific Enforcement
what_happened: For EU-regulated CASPs, the designation of a Kyrgyz A7A5 exchange, the focus on Russia-adjacent financial institutions, and the first use of the anti-circumvention tool are all strong signals that supervisors will expect sanctions-evasion risk to be built into AMLR-driven, harmonized CDD/EDD frameworks by the time the rules bite.

initiative: Investment funds' de facto currency risk exposure
what_happened: Investment Funds' De Facto Currency Risk Exposure A BIS research bulletin published in April 2026 introduces a return-sensitivity framework to measure the actual, rather than declared, currency hedge ratios of investment funds, finding materially different behaviour between asset classes. Bond funds maintain consistently high hedge ratios, though these show some responsiveness to the cost of hedging, while equity funds exhibit volatile, opportunistic positioning that functionally resembles active currency speculation rather than systematic risk management.

initiative: Kalshi flags more insider trading cases, including politician who appeared on FBoy Island
what_happened: Kalshi, the CFTC-regulated prediction market exchange, publicly disclosed three additional insider trading enforcement actions against political candidates who placed bets on their own electoral outcomes — a practice the platform classifies as a direct violation of its rulebook given candidates' ability to influence contract results by choosing whether to enter or exit a race. The cases involved a Virginia U.S. Senate candidate, a Minnesota state lawmaker running for Congress, and a Texas congressional candidate, with penalties ranging from $540 to $6,229 in fines plus five-year trading suspensions and profit disgorgement. Two cooperated and received reduced penalties, while the Virginia candidate publicly defied the process and received the stiffest sanction.

initiative: Bitcoin tops $79,000 as crypto rally gathers steam; Circle, Coinbase, Strategy lead
what_happened: Bitcoin reached an 11-week high above $79,000 on April 22, driven by a combination of improving macro sentiment following President Trump's Iran ceasefire extension and technical positioning dynamics in derivatives markets. Perpetual swap funding rates fell to near three-year lows while open interest simultaneously rose, a configuration K33 Research's Vetle Lunde flagged as indicative of concentrated short buildup in leveraged positions — conditions that historically precede a mechanically driven short squeeze. Crypto-linked equities moved sharply in sympathy, with Strategy gaining 10% as its BTC-heavy balance sheet amplified the move, while Circle Internet and Coinbase rose 9% and 6% respectively ahead of Circle's anticipated public listing.

initiative: The London P2P sweep: UK FCA raids eight illegal peer-to-peer trading hubs
what_happened: The FCA, operating jointly with HMRC and the South West Regional Organised Crime Unit, executed simultaneous raids across eight London locations suspected of running unregistered crypto peer-to-peer trading operations — marking the regulator's first coordinated enforcement action of this type. Each site received a cease-and-desist notice, and seized evidence is now underpinning multiple active criminal investigations focused on money laundering exposure. The action carries particular regulatory weight given that the FCA has confirmed zero registered P2P crypto traders currently exist in the UK, meaning all such operations are by definition unlawful under the existing AML registration framework.

initiative: UK FCA carries out first crackdown on illegal peer-to-peer crypto trading
what_happened: The FCA conducted coordinated physical inspections of eight London premises suspected of operating unregistered peer-to-peer crypto trading activity, working alongside HM Revenue & Customs and the South West Regional Organised Crime Unit. Cease-and-desist letters were issued at each site, and evidence gathered is now feeding into active criminal investigations — a notable escalation given that the regulator has confirmed zero FCA-registered peer-to-peer crypto traders or platforms currently exist in the UK. The action is grounded in existing AML registration requirements that already apply to certain crypto activities ahead of the broader regulatory regime expected to go live in October 2027, meaning firms cannot defer compliance on the basis that the full framework is incomplete.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with former employee of First Financial Bank
what_happened: Federal Reserve Enforcement Action Against Former First Financial Bank Employee The Federal Reserve Board issued a consent prohibition order on April 22, 2026, against Destiny Lara, a former employee of First Financial Bank, headquartered in Abilene, Texas. The action stems from findings of breach of fiduciary duty and bribery, two serious violations that typically indicate insider misconduct involving the misuse of a position of trust for personal gain. Consent prohibition orders bar the named individual from participating in the affairs of any federally regulated financial institution, making this a career-ending sanction in the banking sector.

initiative: Strategy overtakes BlackRock IBIT in bitcoin holdings after bear market buying
what_happened: Strategy Reclaims Top Bitcoin Holder Position from BlackRock IBIT Strategy has accumulated approximately 80,000 BTC in 2026 alone — largely during a period when bitcoin fell more than 50% from its October all-time high — pushing its total holdings to 815,061 BTC and edging past BlackRock's IBIT, which holds 802,824 BTC. The accumulation was funded primarily through a combination of at-the-market equity issuance, convertible debt, and perpetual preferred securities (STRC), the last of which has become a scalable, recurring capital source that distinguishes Strategy's model from passive ETF structures.

initiative: UK invites crypto giant Bybit to London to win over some of UAE’s innovation shine
what_happened: UK government-linked economic development officials formally invited Bybit CEO Ben Zhou to London during UK Fintech Week, arranging meetings with the Financial Conduct Authority and members of the House of Lords to discuss the country's forthcoming crypto regulatory framework and potential establishment of a Bybit presence in the jurisdiction. The outreach is explicitly framed around competitive positioning against the UAE, which has attracted both Bybit—headquartered in Dubai since 2022—and Binance, which relocated there in 2025, creating a cluster effect that drew smaller crypto firms to the region.

initiative: U.S. crypto adoption is rebounding, bitcoin still dominates, Deutsche Bank says
what_happened: Deutsche Bank published findings from a 3,400-person retail survey across the U.S., U.K., and EU showing U.S. crypto participation rebounded to 12% in March 2026 from a February trough of 7%, recovering to levels last seen in mid-2025, though the survey's all-time high remains just 14% since tracking began in 2023. The recovery coincided with approximately $1.3 billion in net inflows into U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs during March, suggesting institutional and retail demand are moving in parallel after a soft start to the year. Despite the participation rebound, consumer price sentiment is bearish, with a majority of U.S. respondents expecting bitcoin to trade below current ~$75,000 levels by year-end and only 3% anticipating a return to the late-2025 peak above $120,000.

initiative: Spot bitcoin ETFs log nearly $1 billion in weekly inflows, highest since mid-January
what_happened: U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs collectively attracted $996.4 million in net inflows during the week ending April 20, 2026, the strongest weekly figure since mid-January and the third consecutive week of positive flows, bringing the three-week cumulative total above $1.8 billion. BlackRock's IBIT dominated activity, accounting for $906 million of the weekly total, consistent with its position as the largest bitcoin ETF by net assets. Morgan Stanley's recently launched MSBT — which debuted April 8 and is notable as a wirehoused, advisor-distributed product rather than a direct retail vehicle — completed its first full trading week with $71 million in net inflows and no daily outflow sessions since launch.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement action with Community Bankshares, Inc.
what_happened: Federal Reserve Issues Cease and Desist Order Against Community Bankshares, Inc. The Federal Reserve Board executed a formal Cease and Desist Order dated April 14, 2026 against Community Bankshares, Inc., a bank holding company headquartered in LaGrange, Georgia, with the action publicly announced two days later. A Cease and Desist Order is among the more serious enforcement tools available to the Fed, legally compelling the institution to halt specific unsafe or unsound practices and requiring affirmative corrective measures under threat of further penalty.

initiative: Bitcoin ETFs add $186M as Morgan Stanley’s MSBT inflows top $100M in first six trading days
what_happened: On April 16, 2026, U.S. spot bitcoin ETFs collectively attracted $186.1 million in net inflows for the session, with BlackRock's IBIT accounting for the bulk at $291.9 million while offsetting redemptions from Fidelity's FBTC, ARK/21Shares' ARKB, and Grayscale's GBTC. The session brought cumulative net inflows across the 13-fund cohort to approximately $57.1 billion since the January 2024 launch, with total net assets representing 6.5% of bitcoin's market capitalization. Morgan Stanley's MSBT, which launched under the firm's registered investment adviser and broker-dealer distribution infrastructure, crossed $103 million in cumulative net inflows within six trading days—surpassing the entire lifetime total of WisdomTree's BTCW, a fund that has operated considerably longer.

initiative: Bitcoin capped at $75,000 as Nasdaq and S&P 500 fly to new record highs
what_happened: Bitcoin has been unable to sustain a break above the $75,000 level, with the most recent attempt on April 15 stalling at the same technical threshold that has capped price action for roughly two months, leaving the asset approximately 40% below its October 2025 peak. The divergence with U.S. equities is operationally notable: the Nasdaq closed above 24,000 for its 11th consecutive record session and the S&P 500 cleared 7,000, suggesting macro risk appetite is not the binding constraint on bitcoin's underperformance. Crypto-adjacent equities—Coinbase (+6.2%), Robinhood (+10%), and Strategy (+4.4%)—participated in the broader rally, indicating that institutional exposure is rotating into listed proxies rather than spot BTC.

initiative: FSB Chair warns of rising financial risks stemming from Middle East conflict
what_happened: FSB Chair Issues Pre-G20 Warning on Conflict-Driven Systemic Risk In a formal letter addressed to G20 Finance Ministers and Central Bank Governors ahead of their April 16 meeting, FSB Chair and Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey outlined how ongoing Middle East conflict is compounding pre-existing structural vulnerabilities across global financial markets. Bailey specifically flagged the risk of simultaneous crystallisation across three interconnected pressure points: leveraged government bond basis trades concentrated among a small number of non-bank funds, stretched asset valuations in sectors including AI, and deteriorating credit quality within opaque private credit markets where contagion risk is amplified by limited transparency.

initiative: Supervising DEI in insurance - Executive Summary
what_happened: IAIS Application Paper: DEI Integration into Insurance Supervision Frameworks The Financial Stability Institute has summarised a new IAIS Application Paper that formally embeds diversity, equity, and inclusion considerations into the supervisory assessment of insurer governance and risk management, operating under the existing Insurance Core Principles — specifically ICP 7 on corporate governance and ICP 8 on risk management and internal controls. The paper establishes that deficient DEI practices constitute a concrete prudential risk vector, with identified exposures spanning governance quality, legal liability, reputational standing, and talent retention, rather than treating DEI as a standalone conduct or social obligation.

initiative: Tiff Macklem: Release of the Monetary Policy Report
what_happened: Bank of Canada Holds Rate at 2.25% Amid Middle East-Driven Energy Shock At its April 29, 2026 decision, the Bank of Canada's Governing Council held the overnight policy rate steady at 2.25% — unchanged since October 2025 — as policymakers weighed competing pressures from an energy-driven inflation spike against softening domestic demand and trade headwinds from U.S. tariffs. The escalation of the Middle East conflict has driven global oil prices to approximately USD 90/barrel, pushing Canadian CPI from 1.8% in February to 2.4% in March, with the Bank projecting a peak near 3% in April before a return to the 2% target by early 2026, contingent on oil prices declining to around USD 75/barrel.

initiative: Aleš Michl: A central bank. And bitcoin.
what_happened: Czech National Bank Governor Aleš Michl delivered a formal speech at The Bitcoin Conference 2026 in Las Vegas, marking a notable instance of a sitting G10-adjacent central bank governor directly engaging a crypto-native audience at an industry event. The speech framed the CNB's post-2022 monetary tightening—which successfully reduced inflation from near 20% to the 2% target—as a philosophical foundation for evaluating Bitcoin alongside traditional reserve management principles. Michl positioned the CNB's approach as structurally hawkish and pro-saving, implicitly signaling openness to hard-asset or scarcity-based monetary concepts consistent with that framework.

initiative: Jorgovanka Tabaković: Brave old world

The speech used the report's demographic focus as a platform to articulate the NBS's institutional view that declining fertility, labour force contraction, and population ageing represent primary structural risks to GDP growth, fiscal sustainability, and inflation dynamics across both Serbia and the broader European transition region.

initiative: Joachim Nagel: Already here, not yet everywhere - shaping the economic impact of artificial intelligence
what_happened: Bundesbank President Joachim Nagel delivered a keynote address at the International Economic Symposium in Rome on 21 April 2026, setting out his institutional view on how artificial intelligence is likely to reshape growth, inflation dynamics, and financial stability — the three core variables central banks monitor for policy calibration. He drew an explicit parallel to the slow-burn productivity diffusion seen during electrification, while arguing AI may propagate faster than prior general-purpose technologies, implying a potentially compressed window for policymakers to assess and respond to structural economic shifts.

initiative: Anna Breman: Global shockwaves to Kiwi shores - the impact of the Iran conflict on New Zealand

She flagged that near-term headline inflation is likely to rise while growth momentum softens, though core inflation has held at 2.4% and medium-term expectations remain anchored—circumstances that constrain but do not preclude further OCR adjustments from the current 2.25% level.

He framed the speech around the structural weight of non-banks — which now hold roughly half of global financial assets — and Ireland's outsized role as a domicile for European fund activity, with approximately €6 trillion in assets under management.

initiative: Dimitar Radev: The current environment is not defined primarily by the baseline scenario. It is defined by the widening range of outcomes around it
what_happened: Bulgarian National Bank Governor Dimitar Radev delivered a formal macroeconomic assessment to diplomatic mission heads in Sofia on 30 March 2026, framing the current conjuncture not as a deviation from a baseline but as a structural widening of the distribution of plausible outcomes — a distinction with direct implications for how central banks and fiscal authorities calibrate policy buffers. Radev disclosed that the ECB Governing Council's March 18–19 meeting specifically modelled Middle East conflict transmission into euro area inflation via energy prices, concluding that short-term effects are manageable but medium-term persistence remains contingent on conflict duration and second-round pass-through.

initiative: ESMA clarifies expectations in the run-up to the launch of EU’s Consolidated Tapes
what_happened: ESMA published a Q&A document addressing the onboarding process for data contributors and operational rules governing the two selected Consolidated Tape Providers — fairCT for bonds and EuroCTP for equities — ahead of their formal authorisation. Trading venues and Authorised Publication Arrangements are reminded of their legal obligation under MiFIR to feed data into the CTs from go-live, and ESMA is pressing these contributors to begin technical engagement with the CTPs now, including agreeing on transmission protocols and completing connectivity and end-to-end testing. CTPs are simultaneously required to implement confidentiality and integrity safeguards for any data received during this pre-authorisation preparatory phase.

initiative: Richard Byles: Redefining economic leadership - building resilient financial ecosystems in emerging markets

He disclosed that Jamaica holds a record US$6.8 billion in foreign reserves and confirmed that the central bank has deliberately adopted a calibrated, proportionate adaptation of Basel III standards rather than mechanical full implementation, prioritising liquidity resilience and capital quality improvements suited to the domestic system's structure.

initiative: Dimitar Radev: Economic prospects in an uncertain global environment
what_happened: Bulgarian National Bank Governor Dimitar Radev, speaking at the AmCham Business Breakfast in Sofia on 3 April 2026, laid out the BNB's revised macroeconomic outlook amid energy market disruption stemming from the Middle East conflict, projecting Bulgarian GDP growth of approximately 3.0% for 2026 against an inflation rise to roughly 3.7%, with the bank's internal scenario analysis indicating conditions are tracking closer to its "adverse" rather than baseline path. Radev flagged that reduced fiscal headroom—following several years of drawing down pre-pandemic buffers—limits the state's capacity for broad support measures, shifting a greater share of adjustment onto the private sector in terms of cost management, liquidity discipline, and selective capital allocation.

initiative: ESMA launches a call for evidence on restricted subscription and private credit ratings
what_happened: ESMA Call for Evidence: Restricted Subscription and Private Credit Ratings ESMA has opened a formal call for evidence seeking market input on restricted subscription and private credit ratings — products where access to the rating output is limited to a defined set of contracting parties rather than disclosed publicly. The initiative is directed at understanding use cases, counterparty characteristics, and whether the analytical rigour and governance controls applied to these non-public ratings are meaningfully comparable to those governing publicly disclosed ratings under the CRA Regulation.

initiative: Michael S Barr: Rural communities - worth the investment
what_happened: Federal Reserve Governor Highlights Rural Capital Access and CDFI Role Speaking at a Washington forum focused on rural investment, Fed Governor Michael Barr drew on field visits to underserved communities — including the Mississippi Delta — to underscore persistent gaps in rural financial access and the institutional responses emerging to address them. He cited a concrete example of a struggling community bank donating its branch building to a CDFI to preserve local financial services, and pointed to New Markets Tax Credits as an effective tool in catalyzing private investment in distressed areas. The remarks signal continued Fed-level attention to community reinvestment frameworks, CDFI capacity, and tax credit mechanisms as policy-relevant instruments for rural economic inclusion.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board announces termination of enforcement actions with Crédit Agricole S.A. and Crédit Agricole Corporate and Investment Bank, Mega International Commercial Bank Co., Ltd, and the Goldman Sachs Group, Inc.
what_happened: The Federal Reserve formally terminated three separate cease-and-desist orders on March 25, 2026, each of which had been in place for between eight and eleven years. The Crédit Agricole order, originally issued in October 2015, targeted the French banking group and its corporate and investment banking arm over compliance deficiencies; the Mega International Commercial Bank order from January 2018 covered the Taiwanese state-linked lender's U.S. branch network across New York, Chicago, and Silicon Valley, where it had faced Bank Secrecy Act and anti-money-laundering failures; and the Goldman Sachs order from May 2018 stemmed from governance and risk-management deficiencies tied to the 1MDB scandal.

initiative: Quantum Bayesian inference: an exploration
what_happened: Researchers from the BIS and affiliated institutions published a working paper examining whether quantum computation can be practically applied to Bayesian inference, a statistical method central to economic forecasting, monetary policy modeling, and risk estimation. The paper presents a proof-of-concept algorithm built using IBM's open-source Qiskit framework in Python, demonstrating posterior sampling by encoding probability distributions into quantum states whose measurements yield draws from a discretized posterior. The authors are candid that the approach does not yet outperform established classical methods such as Markov Chain Monte Carlo or particle filtering, with quantum state preparation remaining a significant bottleneck.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board announces it has made the joint findings with the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency required for the OCC to approve a request by Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A., for an exemption under section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act
what_happened: Morgan Stanley Bank, N.A. (Salt Lake City, Utah) sought a regulatory exemption from the affiliate transaction restrictions in Section 23A of the Federal Reserve Act to facilitate an internal corporate reorganization involving its European affiliate, Morgan Stanley Europe SE, based in Frankfurt, Germany. Section 23A normally caps and conditions covered transactions between a bank and its affiliates to limit the risk of a bank subsidizing or being exposed to non-bank entities within the same corporate group. The Fed completed the required joint findings with the OCC — a procedural prerequisite under which the two agencies must concur before the OCC, as Morgan Stanley Bank N.A.'s primary federal regulator, can formally grant the exemption.

initiative: Sound management of third-party risk - Executive Summary
what_happened: BCBS Issues Updated Third-Party Risk Management Principles for Banks The Basel Committee on Banking Supervision published a new set of principles governing how banks must manage risks arising from third-party service provider (TPSP) arrangements, formally superseding the 2005 Joint Forum outsourcing paper and updating the framework to reflect the significantly expanded role vendors, cloud providers, and other external parties now play in core banking operations. The twelve principles follow the full lifecycle of a TPSP engagement — from initial risk assessment and due diligence through contracting, onboarding, ongoing monitoring, and planned or unplanned exit — with board-level accountability explicitly anchored as the governance starting point.

initiative: Federal Reserve Board issues enforcement actions with former employee of Ally Bank and former employee of Regions Bank
what_happened: The Federal Reserve issued consent prohibition orders against two former bank employees following separate misconduct findings at federally supervised institutions. Lidia Estrada, formerly of Ally Bank's Sandy, Utah operations, was prohibited after falsifying documents in connection with a request for increased compensation, while Brenda Fuson, formerly of Regions Bank in Birmingham, Alabama, faced prohibition for misappropriating customer funds. Both actions were resolved via consent orders, meaning the individuals agreed to the prohibitions without admitting or denying findings, and bar them from future participation in the affairs of any federally insured depository institution.
